title: Problem parsing verilated.h
body:
---


Author Name: **Amir Gonnen**
Original Redmine Issue: 299 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2010-11-03
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

On verilated.h appears the following code:

```
// Optimization bug in GCC 2.96 and presumably all-pre GCC 3 versions need this workaround
#if 1 //defined(__GNUC__) && __GNUC__ < 3
static inline IData  VL_UNARYMIN_I(IData data) { return -data; }
static inline QData  VL_UNARYMIN_Q(QData data) { return -data; }
#else
1. define VL_UNARYMIN_I(IData data) (-(data))
1. define VL_UNARYMIN_Q(QData data) (-(data))
#endif

```

When this code is parsed by some tools (the dependency checker of Incredibuild in our case) it complains about the macro parameters in @VL_UNARYMIN_I(IData data)@.
This code never compiles because it's on the @#else@ of @#ifdef 1@, but still this appears to disturb other tools that parse this h file.
Is it possible to entirely remove this disabled piece of code in future versions?

Thanks,

Amir
